Tesla, Inc. Form 10-K — Annual Report — Fiscal Year Ended December 31, 2024

This Annual Report on Form 10-K contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995.

Item 1. Business

We design, develop, manufacture, sell and lease high-performance fully electric vehicles and energy generation and storage systems, and offer services related to our products. We generally sell our products directly to customers, including through our website and retail locations.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

Our future growth and competitive position depend on our ability to design and manufacture vehicles and energy products at scale, manage costs effectively, and continue to invest in innovation. We face substantial competition in the automotive and energy markets, particularly from established OEMs that are accelerating their electrification roadmaps.

We may be unable to fully realize the benefits of our investments in artificial intelligence, autonomy, and advanced manufacturing if technology adoption lags our expectations or if regulators in our key markets restrict deployment of autonomous-driving features.

Item 2. Properties

Our manufacturing facilities are located in Fremont, California; Sparks, Nevada; Buffalo, New York; Shanghai, China; Berlin-Brandenburg, Germany; and Austin, Texas.

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis

Total revenues for fiscal year 2024 increased compared to the prior year, driven by growth in vehicle deliveries and energy generation and storage deployments. Operating margin compressed due to price reductions and increased investment in next-generation manufacturing.
